metadata.title: Concept paper on the need for revision of the guideline of medical products used in weight control
metadata.document_type: Concept Paper (not a finalized guideline — identifies topics for future revision of CPMP/EWP/281/96 Rev.1)
metadata.note: This concept paper proposes a revision of the existing weight control guideline (CPMP/EWP/281/96 Rev.1). It identifies regulatory gaps and areas requiring update. Severity ratings are capped at MEDIUM given the non-binding, planning nature of this document. The superseding finalized guideline should be consulted for binding requirements.

rules.rule_text: Weight-reducing medicinal products whose mechanism of action may have a potentially negative influence on blood pressure, heart rate, or other cardiovascular parameters must at minimum exclude a harmful cardiovascular effect before licensing.
rules.verbatim_quote: Considering that the mechanism of action of some weight reducing agents may have a potentially negative influence on blood pressure, heart rate or other cardiovascular parameters, it would be essential to at least exclude a harmful effect before licensing.
rules.exceptions: Products without CV mechanism concerns may follow meta-analytic approach.

rules.rule_text: Neuropsychiatric effects of centrally acting weight-reducing medicinal products must be carefully described and monitored during clinical trials, and proposals for risk minimising measures must be developed, given the increased risk of depressive conditions in subjects with obesity.
rules.verbatim_quote: Considering that the risk of depressive conditions is increased in subjects with obesity, it is of utmost importance that the neuropsychiatric effects of the medical product are carefully described and monitored during the trials and that proposals for risk minimizing measures are developed.
rules.exceptions: Primarily applies to centrally acting agents targeting serotonin and catecholamine pathways.

rules.rule_text: Clinical studies for weight control medicinal products must have a minimum duration of 1 year; the need for longer studies and randomized withdrawal design to evaluate maintenance of weight reduction should be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: Obesity is recognized as a chronic clinical condition that usually requires long-term therapy to induce and maintain weight loss. In the current guideline, a study duration of 1 year is expected, but it could be discussed if longer studies are needed, as well as the need for a randomized withdrawal design to fully evaluate the capability of the medical product to maintain weight reduction.

rules.rule_text: Assessment of cardiovascular safety for weight control products must be aligned with requirements for antihypertensive, glucose-lowering, and lipid-lowering medicinal products, specifying different requirements depending on mechanism of action and preclinical/clinical findings.
rules.verbatim_quote: Requirements concerning the assessment of cardiovascular safety need to be included in line with the existing or pending updates of some other guidelines such as for glucose and lipid lowering medicinal products.
rules.exceptions: Requirements adapted for different target populations and treatment objectives.

rules.rule_text: Assessment and monitoring of neuropsychiatric safety, especially depressive syndromes, must be included and expanded in clinical trials for weight control medicinal products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Requirements concerning the assessment and of neuropsychiatric safety, especially depressive syndromes, need to be updated and expanded.

rules.rule_text: Elderly patients must be adequately represented in clinical trials for weight control medicinal products, in line with the EMA geriatric work plan.
rules.verbatim_quote: The EMA geriatric work plan aims to increase the representation of elderly patients in clinical trials. One tool to achieve this is to specify requirements in disease specific guidelines. This would be relevant also for the guideline on medical products used for weight control.

rules.rule_text: A 5% weight reduction may be considered as a valid primary endpoint for clinical trials of anti-obesity products, subject to regulatory discussion and alignment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Even though intervention studies examining the association of weight reduction and cardiovascular events are lacking, obesity is generally accepted as a surrogate marker for increased cardiovascular risk. In this context, it should be discussed if a 5% weight reduction could be accepted as a primary endpoint.
rules.exceptions: Under discussion; pending finalisation in revised guideline.
